blockers.suggestion: Use a GPU-enabled Python environment with CuPy installed and matching CUDA runtime support.
Build or install the PyQUDA core bindings in this Python environment before runtime proof.
Expose the local PyQUDA checkout on PYTHONPATH or install pyquda_utils into the runtime environment.
Check that pyquda_utils is importable and that the built core helpers are present.

next_actions: Use a GPU-enabled Python environment with CuPy installed and matching CUDA runtime support.
Build or install the PyQUDA core bindings in this Python environment before runtime proof.
Expose the local PyQUDA checkout on PYTHONPATH or install pyquda_utils into the runtime environment.
Check that pyquda_utils is importable and that the built core helpers are present.
Re-run this check with --use-repo-pythonpath if you want to test whether the local ~/PyQUDA checkout is sufficient for import discovery.

repo_pythonpath_diagnostic.detail: Re-run with --use-repo-pythonpath if you want to test whether the local checkout is discoverable without installation.

shortest_remaining_steps: Install CuPy into the target Python environment.
Build or install pyquda into the target Python environment.
Install the PyQUDA Python package or make the checkout importable in upstream-supported dev mode.
Ensure pyquda_utils.core is importable after installing/building PyQUDA.

submission_checklist.detail: One or more required runtime modules are missing from the current Python environment.
Did not modify PYTHONPATH for this probe.
